name: Whisparr Sync
description: Connects Cove to a Whisparr v3 (Eros) or v2 instance. Import, reconciliation, and the read-only Whisparr-status views work on both versions; the outward monitor / add / search / bulk controls work on both too, keyed on the id each version carries (StashDB on v3, ThePornDB on v2). On a v2 connection a studio monitors as its site (series): the extension adds it non-grabbing via POST /api/v3/series, flips monitored via PUT /api/v3/series/{id}, and searches its episodes via POST /api/v3/command (EpisodeSearch). Performer monitoring and the per-scene push controls have no v2 counterpart and are disabled with a neutral note (no wire call). OUTBOUND: makes authenticated HTTP calls to the Whisparr URL you configure (default port 6969) with an API key you supply — a Test connection (GET /api/v3/system/status), root-folder/quality-profile reads, a movie/history read for reconciliation, and a best-effort webhook auto-register (POST /api/v3/notification). On a v2 connection those same connect/reconcile reads enumerate series/episodes instead of movies (GET /api/v3/series, /api/v3/episode, /api/v3/episodefile). When you toggle Monitor on a studio or performer it also mutates Whisparr: on v3 it reads-or-creates the movie/studio/performer entity monitored:false (applying your configured root folder and quality profile and a "cove-sync" origin tag it reads-or-creates via GET/POST /api/v3/tag), then PUTs to set the monitored state; on v2 the same add-then-flip applies to the site (series) resolved by the studio's ThePornDB id. It never triggers a Whisparr search. SCENE & BULK MUTATIONS (v3): the same origin-tagged, searchForMovie:false pattern applies at the scene level — adding a scene (Add to Whisparr) and "Add all missing" for a studio/performer register scenes as movies via POST /api/v3/movie with monitored/searchForMovie:false (never grabbing), monitor/unmonitor a scene reads-or-adds then PUTs /api/v3/movie/{id}, and excluding a scene from Whisparr writes an import-list exclusion via POST /api/v3/exclusions while un-excluding removes it via DELETE /api/v3/exclusions/{id} (both idempotent and origin-safe; the exclusion id is resolved server-side by matching the scene's StashDB id, never a caller-supplied id, and an exclusion never grabs). The only grab-capable actions are the explicit "Search now", "Search all monitored", and "Search for upgrades" (POST /api/v3/command, MoviesSearch — an upgrade grabs only a monitored, cutoff-unmet movie) and the interactive "Grab this release" that downloads one user-chosen indexer release (POST /api/v3/release); each is user-initiated. Any grab that results imports back only through the existing On-Import webhook (there is no second ingest path). READ-ONLY STATUS: to show each scene's Whisparr status in Cove it also READS from Whisparr — GET /api/v3/movie and GET /api/v3/exclusions for the library summary and the per-scene detail panel, and (only when you explicitly expand a scene's releases) GET /api/v3/release for that one movie; these are reads and grab or download nothing. INBOUND: exposes a token-gated webhook receiver at POST /api/extensions/com.alextomas955.whisparrsync/webhook that accepts Whisparr On-Import events and imports the finished file into Cove in place (never moving or deleting Whisparr's files); the shared webhook secret authenticates every request (an unsigned or wrong-token request is rejected). The Whisparr API key and the webhook secret are stored server-side only, never returned to the browser and never written to logs. All settings endpoints are mounted under /api/extensions/com.alextomas955.whisparrsync. Runs no scraper or downloader code.
author: alextomas955
